title: Accessibility Standards for Universal Changing Stations

comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: I am in favor of utilizing the existing the existing standards in the 2024 IBC, Section 1110.4 - 1110.4.4, and ANSI 117.1-2017 section 613 Adult Changing Stations as a starting point for the standard required for airport restrooms. I believe the following additions to these standards should be made: 1. Require a wheelchair turning radius 67’’ minimum outside...

I am the mom of a wheelchair-bound 100lb son with special needs. He is 18, and wears diapers. I cannot change my son on a baby changing table as he is too heavy for those, and they are out in the open which is not appropriate for an 18 year old young man. Right now, I either use a family changing room, when one is available, or have to try to change him in a...

I am an Occupational Therapist specializing in complex disability, particularly cerebral palsy, brain injury, and spinal cord injuries. Most of my patients use wheelchairs. I am also mother to as 28 yo son with quadriplegia. This means that he cannot usefully move his arms or his legs. He requires my assistance to get in and out of his wheelchair, use the to...

Question 1 Develop a new, original federal standard, informed by ICC A117.1-2017 but not limited by it. Current market performance highlights systemic issues with existing implementations, particularly: Poor durability of motorized adjustable height units, often failing within 1.5 to 3 years Lack of accountability for maintenance and repair costs Limited ava...

Access to Universal Changing Tables is limited and needs to be addressed. 1. Universal changing tables could easily take the place of baby changing tables. You could incorporate into the design adjustments to allow for babies and toddlers to be securely fastened into these tables. 2. Universal changing tables need to be low to the ground to allow for ease of...

In response to Question 5 under Section A, I strongly support requiring universal changing tables to have non‑porous surfaces. This requirement is essential for maintaining the safe and sanitary environments that the FAA and Access Board intends to create through this initiative. Cleanliness is one of the most critical factors in protecting both individuals ...

I am commenting on the request seeking public comment on our approach to developing technical standards for universal changing stations required to be provided by sponsors of medium and large hub airports seeking airport development project grants. The IBC Code ICC 1110.4 spells this out nicely. More detail is at NH RSA 155:80 Universal Changing Station Requ...

The Access Board’s effort to develop accessibility standards for universal changing stations is an important step toward improving access and dignity for individuals with disabilities who require caregiver assistance. The proposed rulemaking appropriately recognizes that many individuals with disabilities cannot safely use standard restroom facilities when t...

Hello, I am a disabled parent of a disabled child who is incontinant, and requires adult changing stations. Please also ensure that these tables can have an inclination on the back area. My child cannot safely lay flat due to aspiration risks. There also needs to be a way for the table to go up and down remotely for height specifications. Please ensure there...

Hello, Please find attached a public comment submitted by Iris Mehler, MS, CRC, Co-Executive Director and Co-Founder of 1in6 Support, regarding Docket No. ATBCB-2026-0001: Universal Changing Tables - Technical Accessibility Standards.

Good Morning I wanted to reach out to submit public comment regarding the Adult Changing tables. I am a mother to 4 boys. Three are triplets with disabilities. Two of them are in wheelchairs and would benefit so much from having changing tables at the airports long with other public locations. We have had to resort in changing our 19-year-old sons on floors ...

ATBCB-2026-0001-0048.title: Comment from Southwest Louisiana Independence Center

comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: automatic height adjustability would be key. Also, plenty of turning space is needed because many people who require the use of a changing table have very large sophisticated wheelchairs. Minimally, 6 square feet would be appreciated. A 36” inch wide door is also needed to accommodate larger chairs. Careful thought must be given as to how to dispose of diape...

This would make such a difference in our lives if this was a requirement in public places. My son is 15 years old, 220 lbs. He has a rare genetic disorder and is incontinent. I cannot even think about lifting him up to change him in public and floors and toilet are not a sanitary or fair option. A universal changing table that he could put himself on would h...

I am emailing regarding ATBCB-2026-0001 and my support of increasing requirements for accessible changing tables and bathrooms in airports and all other public spaces as applicable. I have a child with disabilities and we have profound difficulties traveling due to the lack of accessibility with restrooms. It’s not appropriate, nor safe, to have people chang...

Universal changing tables are a necessity for adults and children with disabilities. My adult daughter is disabled with multiple chronic illnesses. Lack of changing tables keeps us at home. Is difficult to even take a day trip. Adjustable height tables are the best but are can have mechanical problems .Can do stationary tables but perhaps no higher than 4 ft...

While an adjustable changing table would be ideal, the reality is that they break. Unfortunately, this is often due to vandalism, but normal wear and tear can cause the same situation. My family recently visited a museum that advertised having a universal changing room, but when we arrived, we found it was closed indefinitely because the table was broken and...

It is long overdue that we address the reality of traveling with a disability. Forcing individuals to use a bathroom floor for changing isn't just a hygiene issue; it’s a fundamental violation of human dignity. Establishing clear, technical standards for height-adjustable tables and proper maneuvering space will ensure that airports are actually inclusive, r...

Accessible changing tables for all public spaces that follow ADA provisions is necessary for many to access these spaces. It ensures basic human biologoical needs (toileting) are met for all. I have a very close friend that limits her travel with her son who has special needs because of the lack of accessible areas like these.

I fully support this regulation. It is a travesty that I’ve visited more U.S. airports with pet relief areas than with universal changing tables. Disabled American travelers deserve access to airport restroom facilities that are sanitary, dignified, and most importantly safe.

How can they get taken care of without universal changing tables? You can't and shouldn't change them in public, without universal changing tables, they can't get their needs met and are therefore secluded from so many things.

I have a child with disabilities and although this no longer affects him at one point it did. I remember very vividly changing him on the floor of a bathroom in an airport on the way to a family members funeral. You see my son was too big for a typical changing table that is in the bathrooms currently. My son has an overgrowth syndrome, which made him a very...

I support Universal Changing Stations in airports, libraries, hospitals, movie theaters, schools, restaurants, etc., etc! To know you (or your child) is 100% included in every aspect of an everyday event or outing is something people shouldn’t have to worry about. It’s something that disabled people have to think about every time they leave their house. From...

We must recognize the need and dignity of providing adult sized changing tables with safety rails in our airports and other public venues. We can certainly afford to meet this need in a safe and sanitary accommodation. Make it so!
As a side note; baby changing tables can only be used for babies. Adult changing tables can be used for people of all sizes--Universal Design in a nutshell.

approval for enhancing the capabilities of changing stations. As the upgraded materials support more enhanced human proportions. As we increase in weight obesity and other major health issues enhancing and enlarging changing stations is highly agreeable to caregivers and other disabled people. Because of the larger size Mobility of other people can do it the...
I think that public adult changing stations should be the default bathroom in airports for people with disabilities.

providing a universal changing room / table would be a huge step to allowing us to travel. Right now the thought of traveling is overwhelming without having to plan on changing my son on the floor. might be a good idea to have a hoyer lift as well in the room.

My comment on Universal Changing Tables is they are going to cost a lot of money that we don’t have. My local airport has one installed at great expense, and it appears to never be used. Thank you for your thoughtful consideration of the National Deficit.

I am the parent of a 17 year old boy who has cerebral palsy amd Down syndrome. He is non-ambulatory and not toilet trained. I have had trouble for the last several years changing his diaper in public places. He outgrew baby changing tables serval years ago. The only way to change him now is by laying him down on the dirty floor. I have to bring towels or a b...

Absolutely this should be a requirement. As a mom to a medically fragile wheelchair bound girl I can’t express how much it means when a place has a space available to humanity change my daughter. As a nurse practitioner and nurse I think it is our responsibility to care for each individuals basic needs with dignity such as toileting.

The UK has an already come up with a brilliant standard, Changing Places Toilets. It is an excellent starting point, with all the regular equipment of a restroom, plus space for two carers, a ceiling lift, an adult sized changing table, and a privacy screen to enable privacy for the disabled person and for carers who need to use the facility whilst keeping t...

I think adult changing stations should be put into any and every public establishment. From medical offices/buildings to public event spaces, motels, airports, etc. I would place them in men's and women's restrooms. They don't necessarily have to be in a stall they could be on the wall near the regular changing station... .or if you could make a standard tha...

Hello, my comment around universal changing tables is to consider its location. Often times, options for changing individuals are in a single use stall meant for families. While these locations are clearly marked and there is a specific use, often times, these locations are used by other individuals who do not need the services. I've unfortunately found empl...
This update is long overdue. Current standards only accommodate infants and very small children, eliminating the use of public and private spaces by those with disabled and their families.
Having a child with special needs that is 7 years old and in diapers this would be amazing and so helpful

Older children and adults with disabilities need safe places to have their needs met when out and about! The time to care is now, before it’s you or your loved one with no place to be cared for in public other than a dirty bathroom floor

I support this effort enthusiastically. My daughter is 22 and requires such spaces to be changed safely, hygienically, and in dignity. As her caregiver, I require this kind of space to help her without a second person— to lift her or place her on the floor alone is nearly impossible, besides awful. The lack of public places to change essentially makes public...

Accessible changing tables for all public spaces that follow ADA provisions is necessary for many to access these spaces. It ensures basic human biologoical needs ( toileting) are met for all. I am optimistic that this will be done.

This is critical for so many individuals and their families to have a safe and reasonably clean space to change as individuals with a variety of needs require this type of toileting support. We have these everywhere for babies to toddlers - lets meet he full population now.
